data.title: Why This Top Analyst Says Buy These 5 Chip Stocks for 2026 — and Urges Caution on Intel and AMD
data.description:
Bernstein believes the AI chip rally still has more room to run. In a new note, top semiconductor analyst Stacy Rasgon said there is "little reason to change our broad perspective," as AI spending remains strong and demand for chipmaking equipment continues to improve. In short, Bernstein sees ongoing investment in AI as a durable trend rather than a short-term spike. It is worth noting that Rasgon ranks 120 out of more than 10,000 analysts tracked by TipRanks and has a success rate of 68%.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Against that backdrop, the firm highlighted five U.S. chip stocks it sees as best positioned to benefit through 2026.

  • Nvidia (NVDA): Bernstein's top pick is Nvidia. Rasgon said AI spending "shows no signs of slowing" and added that Nvidia "appears to be pulling farther ahead" of competitors. The firm prefers Nvidia's product roadmap, arguing the company continues to widen its lead in high-end AI workloads.
  • Broadcom (AVGO): Broadcom is Bernstein's second top pick. The firm sees it as a key beneficiary of AI-related demand across networking and custom chips. It also noted that valuations remain attractive as earnings estimates continue to move higher.
  • Applied Materials (AMAT): Bernstein remains bullish on semiconductor equipment makers and rates Applied Materials Outperform. The firm said it would "own semicap even as valuations reach nosebleed levels," citing rising odds of positive revisions to wafer-fab equipment forecasts as AI-driven capacity expands.
  • Lam Research (LRCX): Lam Research also carries an Outperform rating. Bernstein noted that semicap stocks "powered to new highs" in 2025 but believes demand tied to advanced chip manufacturing can continue to support earnings growth.
  • Qualcomm (QCOM): Rounding out the list is Qualcomm. Bernstein said it continues to favor Qualcomm, despite Apple's (AAPL) plans to rely less on Qualcomm modems, as the firm sees improving fundamentals once Apple-related concerns ease..

What Bernstein Is Avoiding

Bernstein is more cautious on other areas of the chip sector. The firm said analog chipmakers look expensive, while a clear recovery is still hard to see. It also said Intel (INTC) fundamentals are "unattractive," even with recent political support. Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) remains rated Market Perform, as Bernstein continues to prefer Nvidia's roadmap and execution.

Which Chip Stock Is the Best Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the seven stocks mentioned above, Nvidia stands out, offering the highest upside at about 43% and carrying a Strong Buy rating. AMD follows with roughly 29% upside and a Strong Buy consensus. Broadcom also holds a Strong Buy rating, with around 30% upside, and ranks highest on the Smart Score, pointing to strong backing from analysts, hedge funds, and broader market signals.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Wedbush's Daniel Ives on Trump's Electricity Policy: A Potential 'Bottleneck' for Big Tech Data Centers
data.description:
Wedbush analyst Daniel Ives views President Donald Trump's initiative to shield Americans from higher electricity bills due to data centers as a double-edged sword. His push to curb data-center electricity costs could both ease regulatory pressure and slow expansion, depending on how policy translates into certainty and cost controls. It relieves a political "headache" for the administration while risking a "larger bottleneck" for Big Tech's rapid data-center expansions.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Trump signaled this stance on Truth Social (DJT), stating that the administration is working with tech firms to make "major changes" so AI data centers do not drive up electricity costs, with a note that these companies should "pay their own way."

Big Tech Steps Up in Response

Microsoft (MSFT) quickly responded with its Community-First AI Infrastructure initiative, pledging that its data centers will not raise consumer electricity prices, curb water use, and replenish more water than they consume. Ives expects similar commitments from other major players amid growing federal, state, and local scrutiny of large-scale buildouts.

He noted that the U.S. is at a pivotal moment in the AI revolution, where energy constraints could slow data center growth if policy and cost pressures intensify. Ives noted this marks the federal government's first explicit position against electricity-cost increases tied to data centers, building on longstanding "pay their own way" demands from state and local governments. Lawmakers must now balance potential tax revenue from these projects against community energy concerns, a tension that could temper expansion pace.

Broader Energy Challenges and AI Race

Ives also highlights an intensifying energy-supply competition with China, which plans heavy investments in power technologies through 2030. In this landscape, Big Tech's energy strategies will require ongoing negotiations with policymakers, as data centers remain essential to AI dominance.

According to Ives, the AI leadership race will persist through the next decade, but clean energy remains a critical constraint. Ives anticipated AI hyperscalers will pursue partnerships, acquisitions of new energy sources, and scaled contracts with clean-energy providers to fuel their ambitions, while managing costs and availability.

Ives cited Meta's (META) energy deployment efforts tied to long-term power agreements and nuclear investments, Alphabet's (GOOGL) push to secure renewable and low-cost energy, and Microsoft's expanded clean-energy commitments for AI infrastructure. The major takeaway is that energy remains the biggest constraint in the AI arms race, and policy moves around electricity costs will shape how quickly and where data centers can be built.

Which Is the Best Artificial Intelligence Stock?

We used the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool for Best Artificial Intelligence Stocks to determine which stock is currently most favored by analysts.

Wall Street has assigned a "Strong Buy" consensus rating on most of the companies, with chip giant Nvidia (NVDA) offering the highest upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: What's Driving Strategy Stock's (MSTR) Rally Today? A Rare Insider Buy Offers a Clue
data.description:
Strategy (MSTR) stock continued to move higher in pre-market trading after extending Tuesday's rally, as investors reacted to a rare insider purchase and renewed focus on the company's Bitcoin exposure. The rebound comes after a steep selloff, with the stock down about 42% over the past three months.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Insider Steps In After Years of Selling

One of the biggest signals came from inside the company. Director Carl Rickertsen bought 5,000 shares of Strategy, according to a January 12 filing with the SEC. The purchase totaled about $779,000, with shares acquired at roughly $156 each.

In recent years, Rickertsen had only sold shares, making this his first open-market purchase since 2022.

MSCI Relief Offers Short-Term Support

The insider buying also comes just days after Strategy avoided a potential setback linked to MSCI. MSCI has been reviewing whether companies with more than 50% of their balance sheet tied to cryptocurrencies should remain eligible for its indices.

Given Strategy's heavy exposure to Bitcoin, the company was at risk. While MSCI has delayed any removal for now, the issue remains under review, offering short-term relief rather than a final resolution.

Strategy Adds to Its Bitcoin Holdings

Separately, Strategy added more Bitcoin to its balance sheet. The company said it bought 13,627 Bitcoin in one week, its largest purchase since July 2025. This brings Strategy's total Bitcoin holdings to nearly 687,410 coins, keeping it the world's largest corporate holder.

As a result, the stock continues to move closely with Bitcoin's price, which remains a key factor investors watch.

Is MSTR Stock a Buy Now?

On TipRanks, Strategy stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 12 Buys and two Hold ratings. The average Strategy price target of $453.17 implies an impressive 162% upside potential from current levels. Meanwhile, MSTR shares have lost 49% over the past year.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Dogecoin Price Finally Wakes Up with 9% Surge, Leaving Recent Downtrend in the Dust
data.description:
On Wednesday, Dogecoin (DOGE-USD) broke out of a weeks-long slump with a sudden 9% price jump. While the broader cryptocurrency market stayed quiet, meme coins like Dogecoin and Pepe saw a wave of new buyers who were looking for fast-moving opportunities. Dogecoin's price climbed to roughly $0.1457, a level it hasn't seen in weeks, proving that the famous Doge still has plenty of fans in the crypto world.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Why Is Dogecoin's Price Moving?

The jump in Dogecoin's price is less about news and more about market psychology. When big coins like Bitcoin don't move much, traders often get bored and look for high-beta assets, which are tokens that move more aggressively. Analysts noted that a "leveraged Dogecoin ETF" was one of the best-performing funds to start the year, showing that people are feeling brave enough to take bigger risks again.

Dogecoin Price Breaks Out the Downtrend

Technically, this move is important because Dogecoin climbed back above its 50-day moving average. This is a line that many traders watch to see if a coin is healthy.

By crossing this line with high trading volume, Dogecoin signaled that the constant selling from December might finally be over. Buyers stepped in during the U.S. trading session to push the price right to the $0.1457 mark, a key psychological barrier.

For this rally to last, Dogecoin needs to stay above the $0.138 level. If it can hold that ground, experts believe it could soon push toward $0.15 or even higher. However, because meme coins are driven mostly by excitement rather than real-world utility, the price can drop just as fast as it rose if the general mood of the market turns sour again.

At the time of writing, Dogecoin is sitting at $0.1457.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: U.S. Stocks Futures Fall as Investors Digest PPI Report, Bank Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell on Thursday as traders reacted to earnings of major banks and the November Producer Price Index (PPI) reading. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.65%, 0.49%, and 0.38%, respectively, at 8:55 a.m. EST on January 14.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The U.S. Producer Price Index (PPI), a key indicator of Consumer Price Inflation (CPI), rose 0.2% in November, below the Dow Jones consensus of 0.3%. On Tuesday, data showed the consumer price index (CPI), a key metric that could influence the Federal Reserve's monetary policy decision making, rose 2.7% at an annual rate in December.

During Tuesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed lower, dragged down by banking stocks. The S&P 500 and the Dow Jones declined 0.19% and 0.80%, respectively, while the Nasdaq Composite slipped 0.10%.

Turning to trending stocks, Wells Fargo (WFC) shares fell about 2% after the bank reported weaker-than-expected Q4 2025 results. Bank of America (BAC) stock was trending lower despite reporting better-than-expected Q4 2025 results. In contrast, Citigroup (C) stock rose 1.4% after reporting upbeat Q4 2025 results.

Also, cybersecurity stocks Palo Alto Networks (PANW), Check Point Software (CHKP), and Fortinet (FTNT) fell after Chinese authorities ordered domestic companies to cease using U.S. and Israel-linked security software.

data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Dow Jones Stumbles on Unexpected PPI Inflation Data
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is set to open Wednesday in negative territory after a higher-than-expected inflation reading.

For November, the Producer Price Index (PPI) grew by 0.2%, rising from 0.1% in October and matching the estimate of 0.2%. On an annual basis, PPI rose by 3% compared to 2.7% during the prior month and topping the estimate of 2.7%. That marks the highest rate of growth since July.

data.title: Infosys Stock Eyes Recovery as Company Raises Sales Forecast on AI Demand
data.description:
Bengaluru-based IT giant Infosys (INFY) has signaled a turning point for the technology sector by increasing its financial targets for the year. Following a period of slow corporate spending, the company announced on Wednesday that it expects its annual revenue to grow by 3% to 3.5%.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The primary driver behind this revised forecast is a change in how global companies are spending their money. For much of 2024 and 2025, high interest rates and geopolitical tension caused banks and retailers to cut back on discretionary IT projects. However, CEO Salil Parekh noted that clients now view Infosys as their "AI partner of choice." Instead of pausing projects, companies are now actively funding AI-driven digital transformations to stay competitive.

Infosys Demonstrates a Strong Deal Pipeline

The company's deal book provides the most educated look at where the industry is headed. In the quarter ending December 31, Infosys secured $4.8 billion in large contract wins, with more than half of those being "net new" business rather than just renewals. This strong pipeline is why management feels confident enough to raise their forecast even though the broader economy remains unpredictable.

Infosys Still Has to Navigate Regulatory Hurdles

Despite the positive sales outlook, Infosys stock has had to absorb some one-time costs. The company reported a ₹1,289 crore ($143 million) expense related to India's new labor codes, which increased liabilities for employee gratuity and leave. Additionally, the industry is bracing for potential changes to the H-1B visa regime in the United States, which could increase hiring costs. However, by focusing on high-margin AI and cloud services, Infosys aims to maintain its operating margins between 20% and 22%.

Is Infosys a Good Stock to Buy?

TipRanks data shows Infosys (INFY) carries a "Hold" consensus rating from 6 Wall Street analysts over the past three months. The breakdown includes one Buy, five Holds, and no Sells.

The average 12-month INFY price target stands at $18.95, implying a modest 8.2% upside from the latest close.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Here's Why High Roller Technologies Stock (ROLR) Rocketed Today
data.description:
High Roller Technologies (ROLR) stock experienced a massive rally on Wednesday after the online gambling company announced a binding strategic partnership agreement with crypto exchange operator Crypto.com.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Under a binding Letter of Intent (LOI), High Roller Technologies will partner with Crypto.com to offer an event-based prediction markets product in the U.S. This will allow users to trade event contracts across various markets, including finance, entertainment, and sports. The two companies also said this partnership will create a user-friendly platform with legal and engaging trades.

High Roller Technologies and Crypto.com are still working out the details of this deal, which means it might not come to fruition. If all goes well, this platform will launch in the first quarter of 2026, with Crypto.com acting as the "exclusive provider of Prediction Contracts across High Roller distribution channels."

High Roller Technologies Stock Movement Today

High Roller Technologies stock was up 379.83% in pre-market trading on Wednesday, following a 17.33% rally yesterday. Investors will note the stock has rallied 70.87% year-to-date but has fallen 46.59% over the past 12 months.

ROLR stock also experienced heavy trading today, as some 17 million shares changed hands. For the record, the company's three month-daily average trading volume is about 13,000 units.
data.author: William White

data.title: C Earnings: Citigroup Stock Pops on Q4 Earnings Beat
data.description:
Shares of Citigroup (C) are rising in pre-market trading after the banking giant beat fourth quarter expectations. Adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $1.81 rose 35% year-over-year and beat the consensus estimate of $1.65. Also, adjusted revenues of $21 billion surpassed the consensus of $20.94 billion.  

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For fiscal 2025, revenues rose 7% to $86.4 billion and adjusted EPS of $7.97 came in significantly higher than the FY24 figure of $5.94.

Details of Q4 and FY25 Results

Citi's quarterly revenues rose 2% year-over-year on a reported basis, led by growth in Banking, Services, U.S. Personal Banking (USPB), and Wealth, with gains mostly offset by weakness in All Other. Excluding the Russia-related notable item, revenues increased by 8%.

Citi had previously disclosed that Q4 results included a notable item of a $1.2 billion pre-tax loss ($1.1 billion after tax) on the planned sale of AO Citibank in Russia. This stemmed from held-for-sale accounting treatment and was recorded in Other revenues.

The bank returned more than $17 billion in capital to shareholders, the highest amount since the pandemic, including $13 billion via share buybacks.

Citi pays a regular quarterly dividend of $0.60 per share, reflecting a current yield of 1.94%.

Is Citigroup Stock a Buy?

Ahead of the Q4 print, analysts remained highly optimistic about Citigroup's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, Citi has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 13 Buys and three Hold ratings. The average Citigroup price target of $125.50 implies 7.9% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, C shares have gained 58.2%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth
